content.claim: Approximately 3,300 Polish schools closed during 2000–2016, with declining fertility and migration cited as primary drivers, combined with economic reasons as direct closure justifications at local level.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Data aggregated at gmina level; individual school decision-making variation not documented
Economic reasons cited as justification may reflect accounting convenience rather than exhaustive causal analysis
Does not isolate effect of structural reforms from pure demographic attrition
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
